name: Heavenly Peach
description: The Heavenly Peaches, also known as Peaches of Immortality, are a central motif in Chinese mythology, symbolizing longevity and immortality. Cultivated in the celestial gardens of Xiwangmu, the Queen Mother of the West, these peaches ripen only once every several thousand years. The consumption of a Heavenly Peach grants the eater extended life, while consuming multiple can bestow true immortality. The peaches are guarded fiercely, and their rarity makes them highly coveted by deities and mortals alike. Legends often involve deities or mythical figures attempting to steal or acquire these peaches, highlighting their significance in the pursuit of eternal life. The size and appearance of the peaches vary in different accounts, but they are consistently described as possessing a radiant glow and an exquisite aroma. The peach tree itself is considered sacred, and its blossoms are also associated with good fortune and prosperity. The Heavenly Peach represents not just physical immortality, but also spiritual enlightenment and transcendence of the mortal realm. The fruit is a symbol of the blessings bestowed upon those who are virtuous and deserving, and its presence often signifies a harmonious balance between the earthly and celestial realms. The most famous story involving the Heavenly Peaches is likely Sun Wukong's (The Monkey King) theft of them, leading to chaos in the heavens and ultimately shaping his destiny. The peaches continue to be a potent symbol in Chinese culture, frequently appearing in art, literature, and religious practices.
subtitle: Fruit of Immortality and Longevity
currentLocation: Xiwangmu's garden in the Kunlun Mountains (mythical location)

keyMyths.title: Sun Wukong's Theft
keyMyths.description: Sun Wukong stole and consumed many Heavenly Peaches, gaining immense power and immortality. This act caused chaos in the heavens and led to his eventual imprisonment under a mountain.

keyMyths.title: Xiwangmu's Banquet
keyMyths.description: Xiwangmu hosts banquets where the Heavenly Peaches are served to honored guests. Receiving a peach from her is a sign of great favor and blessing.

sources.author: Wu Cheng'en
sources.description: Classic novel featuring Sun Wukong's adventures and his theft of the Heavenly Peaches.
sources.text: Classic novel featuring Sun Wukong's adventures and his theft of the Heavenly Peaches.

sources.author: Livia Kohn
